observations.recommendation: 12. We recommended that the Honorable Mayor instruct the concerned offices to faithfully and regularly submit the monthly pre-closing trial balance, together with SAAOB, and necessary supporting schedules, to the Auditor’s Office not later than the 20th day after the end of the month for evaluation and monitoring purposes.

1.9 Hence, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive mandate:
a) The evaluation of composition of the Inventory Committee at the
beginning of the year, if warranted, replace and/or augment the
members with active and trained personnel who can conduct the
physical count effectively;
b) The Inventory Committee to prepare a PIP, to be approved by the
LCE, which shall guide them in conducting and completing the
annual physical count of all PPEs;
c) The Inventory Committee to assign prescribed unique property
numbers on existing PPEs during the physical count and the
Municipal GSO to do the same on newly procured properties and
equipment;
d) The Inventory Committee to identify the actual condition of the
properties counted and include its status in the report;
e) The Inventory committee to submit the RPCPPE within the
deadline set by the Commission to the Auditor’s Office not later
than January 31 of each year;
f) The complete keeping, maintenance and regular reconciliation of
the property cards, and the PPELC with the results of the physical
count; and
g) Lastly, pending the approval of the supplemental guidelines for
COA Circular 2022-004 dated May 31, 2022 on the increase in
capitalization threshold from 15,000.00 to 50,000.00 of tangible
items, we recommended that all existing equipment, especially
those below ₱50,000.00, be identified, immediately assigned with
prescribed unique property numbers, and listed in a schedule, in
preparation for reclassification once the supplemental guidelines
is approved.

2.9 Therefore, we reiterated our prior year recommendations that the
Local Chief Executive enjoin the department heads’ compliance with
the Circular and direct:
a) The Inventory Committee to conduct an extensive inventory of the
road network system under the LGU’s jurisdiction and prepare a
report thereon in accordance with templates provided in COA
Circular No. 2015-008;
b) The Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant
and the Municipal GSO with the complete description and cost
segregation of road components for road projects;
c) The General Services Officer (GSO) to maintain a local road
inventory, road map and keep a complete and updated Local
Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components;
d) The Municipal Accountant to prepare/adjust the Journal Entry
Voucher, as necessary, to record the beginning/adjusted balance
of the local road network and its components in the general ledger
and maintain the Local Road Network Ledger Cards and
subsidiary records for all roads and road components based on
the result of the physical count;
e) The Municipal Accountant to prepare a monthly lapsing schedule
for computation of the depreciation at the end of each year; and
f) The submission of the following to the Auditor’s Office at the end
of the accounting period, preferably not later than January 31 of
each year:
i. Report on Local Road Network by the GSO;
ii. Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System
by the Inventory Committee; and
iii. Disclosure of Local Road Network System in the Notes to
Financial Statements by the Accounting Office.

3.10 Hence, we reiterated our prior year recommendations that the Local
Chief Executive direct:
a) The Accounting Office to take up depreciation on the
undepreciated PPEs and make the corresponding adjustments on
affected asset, expense, and government equity accounts by
drawing necessary Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) to correct the
misstatements;
b) The General Services Office and Accounting Office to strictly
recognize and record depreciation in the property cards and
ledgers for all existing and to be acquired PPEs;
c) The Accounting Office to prepare a monthly schedule of
depreciation for all the PPE assets of the LGU; and
d) The Accounting Office to disclose in the Notes to Financial
Statements the necessary details for each class of PPE reported in
the financial statements, including increases and derecognition
thereof, if any, to to make the financial statements more
comprehensible.

4.10 Thus, we reiterated our previous recommendations that the Local
Chief Executive direct:
a) The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to
submit to the Auditor’s Office a schedule showing all the fund
transfers received by the Management as of date, and another
schedule showing the breakdown of the CIP-Infrastructure
account;
b) The Municipal Accountant to draw the necessary Journal Entry
Vouchers (JEVs) to establish the trust liability account based on
the submitted schedule to correct the ledgers;
c) The Municipal Accountant to keep separate subsidiary records
for each existing fund transfer and for future fund transfers from
sources agencies;
d) The Municipal Accountant to submit the RCIs and RODs related
to the fund transfers, together with its vouchers and supporting
documents to the Auditor’s Office within ten (10) days from the
end of the month;
e) The MPDO to submit to the Auditor’s Office the list of all on-
going and to be implemented PPAs, properly categorized whether
under the General or under the Trust Fund, at the beginning of
the year; and
f) The Municipal Engineer and/or the MPDO to include in the
monthly monitoring report of PPAs the physical and financial
status of projects funded through fund transfers.

5.4 Therefore, we recommended and Management agreed to direct:
a) The Municipal Treasurer shall follow-up and promptly request
from the AGDB the release of bank statements of all accounts
within five (5) days at the end of the month; and
b) Thereupon, the same shall be furnished to the Municipal
Accountant who shall then prepare and submit the BRS to the
Auditor’s Office on or before the 15th day of the ensuing month.
The Accountant should also see to it that prior to submission, the
BRS is supported with bank statement.

6.4 Therefore, we recommended and Management agreed to mandate:
a) The HRMO shall submit a complete listing of all the JOs hired by
the Management, by department, and with information on age
and period of contract;
b) All the JOs shall submit their DTRs or certification of services
rendered duly signed by the immediate supervisor,
Accomplishment Reports duly approved and signed by the
immediate supervisor, and copies of contracts on which the
Statements of Duties and Responsibilities to be attached to and as
basis for the preparation of payroll. Otherwise, payments thereof
maybe suspended or disallowed, if warranted.

We strongly recommended that the Honorable Mayor direct the Accounting Office:
1. To make the necessary adjustments on affected accounts such as PPE asset, expense, and government equity account to lessen the materiality of misstatements by recognizing depreciation expense and related accumulated depreciation account, especially on new constructed and newly procured PPEs.
2. To strictly recognize depreciation in the related property ledgers; and
3. To disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the necessary details for each class of PPE reported in the financial statements, including increases, to support amounts reported in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Honorable Mayor instruct:
8. The Accounting Office to immediately correct the erroneous balances to present fairly the financial statements by drawing the necessary adjusting entries;
9. That the Accounting Office be furnished with copies of the monitoring report on the 20% development by the Office of the Municipal Planning and Development Office, for purposes of reconciliation, especially when preparing trial balances.
Finally, the Auditor commends the MPDO for their regular submission of quarterly reports.
For purposes of improving their report, we recommended that:
10. The remarks column of their report be labeled “COMPLETED” upon completion of the project; and
11. The report be supported with a schedule showing the realigned program/ projects/ activities (PPAs) and the list of PPAs that benefited from the alignment, certified by the MPDO and noted by the Municipal Budget Officer.
13. We recommended that the Honorable Mayor instruct the concerned office to disclose explanation of differences reported in the SCBAA, material or not, in the notes to financial statements.

We recommended that the Honorable Mayor instruct:
14. The immediate submission of the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRM Funds for the months of October to December 2021, list of all PPAs undertaken by the MDRRM Office during the year, and a written letter explaining the reason behind their substantial delay of compliance, to the Auditor’s Office within three (3) days from receipt hereof; and
15. On-time preparation of the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF by the Local Accountant on a monthly basis, duly noted by the MDRRM Officer, and regular submission thereof to the Auditor’s Office on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.
16. We also recommended that the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRM Funds, upon submission by the MDRRM Officer, be supported with documentation of the programs/projects/and activities undertaken such as but not limited to the following: photocopy of the LDRRMF Investment Plan, copies of approved resolution for the release and utilization of QRF and Special Trust Fund, during state of calamities, and photographs of projects/programs/activities undertaken during the month of report.

We recommended and Management concurred that:
17. The immediate reconciliation of the Report on the Sources and Utilization of LDRRM Funds with the SCBAA shall be facilitated, and thereafter make the necessary adjustments in the ledgers/reports, if any.
18. The Budget Office shall furnish the Accounting Office with copies of RAAOs for reconciliation purposes.

observations.managementAction: Management concurred that the concerned officials of the Treasury Office shall:

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management concurred that:
23. Prepare the RCD, complete, and in accordance with the format and manner prescribed in the Manual, and attach its pertinent supporting documents such as duplicates of issued official receipts, community tax certificates, and validated deposit slips;
24. Certify the accuracy and correctness of the collections and deposits and balance of accountable forms reported; and
25. Update the Cashbook – Local Treasury based on the RCD prepared.
26. Thereafter, forward the RCD, together with its supporting documents, to the Accounting Office.
The Accounting Office shall:
27. Acknowledge the receipt of the RCD;
28. Verify the accuracy of computation and check the completeness of the supporting documents; and
29. Prepare the JEVs based on the RCD.
30. Thereafter, submit the RCD, together with its supporting documents, to the Office of the Auditor, duly accompanied with a formal transmittal letter noted by the Municipal Mayor.

We recommended and Management concurred that:
31. Keep and maintain a check register (Annex A), per fund, to monitor release of checks to claimants;
32. Prepare the RCI (Annex B), per fund, daily or as needed, and report the checks, whether released or unreleased, in a chronological order, complete, including the cancelled ones; and
33. Submit the RCI, duly certified by the Municipal Treasurer, including the DVs and their supporting documents, to the Accounting Office.
34. At month end, prepare a schedule of unreleased and cancelled checks (suggested format attached as Annex C), if any, duly certified by the Treasurer, and forward the same to the Accounting Office.
The Accounting Office shall:
35. Faithfully acknowledge receipt of RCI from the Office of the Treasurer;
36. Tally the number of DVs against the number of checks issued reported in the RCI and check the completeness of their supporting documents; and
37. Prepare JEV for each transaction and record the same to its appropriate journals
38. Thereafter, submit the RCI, duly received by the Accounting Office, together with the DVs and their supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor, accompanied by a formal letter of transmittal, signed by the Municipal Accountant and noted by the Honorable Mayor.
observations.managementAction: Management concurred that: The Office of the Treasurer shall:

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management concurred that the Accountable Officers shall:
39. Adopt the cashbook form and template (Annex A – Local Treasury, B – Cash in Bank, and C – Cash Advances) as prescribed in the Manual, for each fund (General Fund, Special Education Fund, and Trust Fund) and for each bank accounts maintained by the LGU;
40. Properly label each cashbook to outrightly determine whether it is Cashbook for Local Treasury, Cash Advances, or Cash in Bank.
41. Regularly update the cashbooks. All totals shall be written legibly in ink, ruled and footed, and the balances shall be carried forward as opening balances of the cashbook for the next month; and
42. Finally, the Accountable Officers shall accomplish the certification as prescribed in the Manual during month ends, directly below the last entry for the month.
observations.managementAction: Management concurred that Accountable Officers shall:

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Honorable Mayor concurred to enjoin the Office of the Treasurer to:
43. Clearly stamp the word “Cancelled” on the face of the cancelled checks; and
44. As recommended in AOM 2021-007 dated December 20, 2021, prepare a schedule of cancelled checks at the end of the month, attach the actual cancelled checks, and regularly submit the same along with the RCI to the Auditor’s Office.
observations.managementAction: We recommended and the Honorable Mayor concurred to enjoin the Office of the Treasurer to:

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management concurred that the Accounting Office shall:
45. Faithfully write on the face of the DV, specifically in Box D, the JEV numbers to indicate preparation thereof; and
46. At least make sure that the JEVs are printed, filed, and submitted to the Auditor’s Office, together with the DVs accompanied by a proper letter of transmittal, if attachment thereof to each DV is still burdensome and time-consuming.
observations.managementAction: Management concurred that the Accounting Office shall:

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management concurred that:
47. The Municipal Treasurer shall observe the stamping of “PAID”, with the prescribed size of 2’’ X 3’’, on all the disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents after payment is made.
48. The number of check issued and the date of the actual payment shall be indicated on the face of each of the supporting documents to avert any attempt to use the same supporting documents in payment of future claims.
49. The Accounting Office shall ensure that all paid DVs, including its supporting documents, pending submission to the Auditor’s Office are stamped “PAID”.

60. The Municipal Mayor shall Require the Disbursing Officers to submit the Report of Disbursements (ROD) pertaining to their cash advances together with all the liquidating instruments every 5th day of the ensuing month pursuant to Section 46 and Annex 46 of the NGAS for LGUs

Hence, we strongly recommended that the Honorable Mayor direct the
Accounting Office:
a) To make the necessary adjustments on affected accounts such as
PPE asset, expense, and government equity account to lessen the
materiality of misstatements by recognizing depreciation expense
and related accumulated depreciation account, especially on new
constructed and newly procured PPEs.
b) To strictly recognize depreciation in the related property ledgers; and
c) To disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the necessary
details for each class of PPE reported in the financial statements,
including increases, to support amounts reported in the financial
statements.

Hence, we recommended that the Honorable Mayor instruct:
a) The Inventory Committee (IC) to immediately adopt the
prescribed form in preparing the RPCPPE;
b) The IC to completely list and properly classify the PPEs according
to its type/category;
c) That upon completion of the physical count, the General Services
Office and Accounting Office undertake collaborative effort to
maintain, update, and reconcile their cards and ledgers with the
results of the physical count; and
d) That identified discrepancies, if any, be properly addressed by
proper recognition or disposition thereof, whichever is applicable.

In view of the foregoing deficiencies, we strongly recommended that the
Honorable Mayor instruct the following:
a) The immediate submission of all un-submitted reports pertaining
to the project namely Concreting of Park Area Access Road to
Sitio Magampong the Auditor’s Office, such as the Report of
Checks Issued (RCI) duly certified by the Municipal Treasurer,
Report of Disbursements (ROD), disbursement vouchers,
payrolls, together with its supporting documents;
b) Initiate the keeping of separate updated subsidiary records for
each transfer and for future transfers from NGAs, whether or not
maintained in separate bank accounts;
c) Make the necessary adjustments, if any, to correct the ledgers,
trial balance, and the financial statements; and
d) Submission to the Auditor’s Office of a schedule showing all the
transfers/subsidies actually received by the Management
indicating the source agency, purpose, amount received, and
balance, if any, as at December 31, 2021 and in the ensuing years.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Honorable Mayor
instruct:
a) The Accounting Office to immediately correct the erroneous
balances to present fairly the financial statements by drawing the
necessary adjusting entries; and
b) That the Accounting Office be furnished with copies of the
monitoring report on the 20% development by the Office of the
Municipal Planning and Development Office, for purposes of
reconciliation, especially when preparing trial balances.
c) Finally, the Auditor commends the MPDO for their regular
submission of quarterly reports. For purposes of improving their
report, we recommended that:
   i. The remarks column of their report be labeled
   “COMPLETED” upon completion of the project; and
   ii. The report be supported with a schedule showing the
   realigned program/projects/activities (PPAs) and the list of
   PPAs that benefited from the alignment, certified by the
   MPDO and noted by the Municipal Budget Officer.

Hence, we recommended that the Honorable Mayor instruct the
concerned offices to faithfully and regularly submit the monthly pre-
closing trial balance, together with SAAOB, and necessary supporting
schedules, to the Auditor’s Office not later than the 20th day after the
end of the month for evaluation and monitoring purposes.

Therefore, we recommended that the Honorable Mayor instruct the
concerned office to disclose explanation of differences reported in the
SCBAA, material or not, in the notes to financial statements.

Hence, we recommended that the Honorable Mayor instruct:
a) The immediate submission of the Report on Sources and
Utilization of LDRRM Funds for the months of October to
December 2021, the list of all PPAs undertaken by the MDRRM
Office during the year, and a written letter explaining the reason
behind their substantial delay of compliance, to the Auditor’s
Office within three (3) days from receipt hereof; and
b) On-time preparation of the Report on Sources and Utilization of
und LDRRMF by the Local Accountant on a monthly basis, duly
noted by the MDRRM Officer, and regular submission thereof to
the Auditor’s Office on or before the 15th day after the end of each
month.
c) We also recommended that the monthly Report on Sources and
Utilization of LDRRM Funds, upon submission by the MDRRM
Officer, be supported with documentation of the
programs/projects/and activities undertaken such as but not
limited to the following: photocopy of the LDRRMF Investment
Plan, copies of approved resolution for the release and utilization
of QRF and Special Trust Fund, during state of calamities, and
photographs of projects/programs/activities undertaken during
the month of report.

Hence, we recommended and Management concurred that:
d) The immediate reconciliation of the Report on the Sources and
Utilization of LDRRM Funds with the SCBAA shall be facilitated,
and thereafter make the necessary adjustments in the
ledgers/reports, if any.
e) The Budget Office shall furnish the Accounting Office with copies
of RAAOs for reconciliation purposes.

Hence, we recommended and Management concurred that the
concerned officials of the Treasury Office shall:
a) Faithfully prepare and accomplish their RCDs, together with its
necessary abstract/schedules and supporting documents, at the
end of the business day;
b) Consolidate the RCDs of all tellers;
c) Duly certify and acknowledge receipt and verification of their
respective RCDs; and
d) Faithfully deposit the receipts and collections, intact, daily to
avoid possible loss and misappropriation.

We recommended and Management concurred that:
a) The Accountable Officer shall:
i) Prepare the RCD, complete, and in accordance with the
format and manner prescribed in the Manual, and attach
its pertinent supporting documents such as duplicates of
issued official receipts, community tax certificates, and
validated deposit slips;
ii) Certify the accuracy and correctness of the collections and
deposits and balance of accountable forms reported; and
iii) Update the Cashbook – Local Treasury based on the RCD
prepared.
iv) Thereafter, forward the RCD, together with its supporting
documents, to the Accounting Office.
b) The Accounting Office shall:
i) Acknowledge the receipt of the RCD;
ii) Verify the accuracy of computation and check the
completeness of the supporting documents; and
iii) Prepare the JEVs based on the RCD.
iv) Thereafter, submit the RCD, together with its supporting
documents, to the Office of the Auditor, duly
accompanied with a formal transmittal letter noted by the
Municipal Mayor.

In view thereof, we recommended and Management concurred that:
a) The Office of the Treasurer shall:
i. Keep and maintain a check register (Annex A), per fund, to
monitor release of checks to claimants;
ii. Prepare the RCI (Annex B), per fund, daily or as needed,
and report the checks, whether released or unreleased, in a
chronological order, complete, including the cancelled ones;
iii. Submit the RCI, duly certified by the Municipal Treasurer,
including the DVs and their supporting documents, to the
Accounting Office.
iv. At month end, prepare a schedule of unreleased and
cancelled checks (suggested format attached as Annex C), if
any, duly certified by the Treasurer, and forward the same
to the Accounting Office.
b) The Accounting Office shall:
i. Faithfully acknowledge receipt of RCI from the Office of the
Treasurer;
ii. Tally the number of DVs against the number of checks
issued reported in the RCI and check the completeness of
their supporting documents; and
iii. Prepare JEV for each transaction and record the same to its
appropriate journals
i. Thereafter, submit the RCI, duly received by the Accounting
Office, together with the DVs and their supporting
documents to the Office of the Auditor, accompanied by a
formal letter of transmittal, signed by the Municipal
Accountant and noted by the Honorable Mayor.

We recommended and Management concurred that Accountable
Officers shall:
(a) Adopt the cashbook form and template (Annex A – Local
Treasury, B – Cash in Bank, and C – Cash Advances) as
prescribed in the Manual, for each fund (General Fund, Special
Education Fund, and Trust Fund) and for each bank accounts
maintained by the LGU;
(b) Properly label each cashbook to outrightly determine whether it
is Cashbook for Local Treasury, Cash Advances, or Cash in Bank.
(c) Regularly update the cashbooks. All totals shall be written legibly
in ink, ruled and footed, and the balances shall be carried forward
as opening balances of the cashbook for the next month; and
d) Finally, the Accountable Officers shall accomplish the
certification as prescribed in the Manual during month ends,
directly below the last entry for the month.

We recommended and the Honorable Mayor concurred to enjoin the
Office of the Treasurer to:
a) Clearly stamp the word “Cancelled” on the face of the cancelled
checks; and
b) As recommended in AOM 2021-007 dated December 20, 2021,
prepare a schedule of cancelled checks at the end of the month,
attach the actual cancelled checks, and regularly submit the same
along with the RCI to the Auditor’s Office.

In view of this, we recommended and Management concurred that the
Accounting Office shall:
a) Faithfully write on the face of the DV, specifically in Box D, the
JEV numbers to indicate preparation thereof; and
b) At least make sure that the JEVs are printed, filed, and submitted
to the Auditor’s Office, together with the DVs accompanied by a
proper letter of transmittal, if attachment thereof to each DV is
still burdensome and time-consuming.

We recommended and Management concurred that:
a) The Municipal Treasurer shall observe the stamping of “PAID”,
with the prescribed size of 2’’ X 3’’, on all the disbursement
vouchers and its supporting documents after payment is made.
b) The number of check issued and the date of the actual payment
shall be indicated on the face of each of the supporting documents
to avert any attempt to use the same supporting documents in
payment of future claims.
c) The Accounting Office shall ensure that all paid DVs, including
its supporting documents, pending submission to the Auditor’s
Office are stamped “PAID”.

The Local Chief Executive shall:
1) Instruct the Inventory
Committee to conduct
immediately an extensive
inventory of the road network
system under the
Municipality’s jurisdiction
and prepare a report thereon;
2) Require the Municipal
Accountant to religiously
update the subsidiary records
for roads and road
components for every road
network based on the result of
the physical count;
3) Further require the Municipal
Accountant to prepare a
lapsing schedule for
computation of the
depreciation at the end of each
year;
4) Instruct the General Services
Officer (GSO)/Supply Officer
to religiously maintain Local
Road Inventory and Road
Map;
5) Likewise, have the GSO/
Supply Officer keep an
updated complete Local Road
Network Property Card for all
roads and its components and
make a reconciliation with
that of the Accounting
records; and
6) Finally, require the Municipal
Engineer to provide the
Accountant and the General
Services Office with the
complete description.

The Management shall:
7) Immediately submit a written
explanation on how the
procurement of goods pushed
through and consequently
paid when ‘no meeting of
minds’ took place as depicted
by the unsigned and
deficiently filled-out POs;
and
8) Henceforth, instruct that POs
must be completely filled-out
with essential information
acknowledged by the supplier
before acceptance and
payment thereof.

The Local Chief Executive shall:
9) Cause the immediate
submission of the copies of
BAC Resolution (if any) and
RFQs, if any, to the audit team for
evaluation of the mode of
procurement availed;
10) If none, submit a written
explanation on noncompliance,
thereof.
11) Henceforth, strictly, support
procurements with approved
BAC resolution and strictly
followed the rules on
preparation of RFQs as
prescribed in IRR of RA 9184.
12) The Local Chief Executive
(LCE) shall cause the
immediate submission of the
approved supplemental AIP
pertaining to the BGCM as
specified in DBM Local Budget
Circulars (LBCs) No.
124 and 125 dated
March 26, 2020 and
April 7, 2020.

The Local Chief Executive shall
direct the Local Finance
Committee:
13) To submit certified true
copies of certifications of
posting requirements by the
BAC Secretariat relative to
the BGCM PPA procurements;
14) Though late, still post the
accumulated reports on the
LGU’s website or Facebook
page and in conspicuous
public places in the locality
for general transparency; and
15) Finally, furnish the audit
team a copy of the written
notice when posting has been
complied, including photos to
validate posting in
conspicuous places in the
locality.

The Local Chief Executive shall:
16) Cause the immediate
submission of all the
deficient documents stated
herein.
17) Henceforth, strictly adhere to
the rules and procedures
prescribed under IRR RA
9184 on agency to agency
procurements.

18) Instruct the GAD Focal
Person to observe timely
submission of GAD Plan and
Budget to DILG Regional
Office for review and
approval in compliance with
PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA
Joint Memorandum Circular
No. 2013-01, and
19) Strictly ensure that the same
will be issued a DILG
certificate of approval upon
return and before
incorporation to the Annual
Budget to be enacted by the
Sanggunian.

The Municipal Mayor shall
require the:
20) Supply Officer to prepare and
maintain complete and
updated Property Cards for
and reconcile their records
regularly with the
Accounting Office.

The Management shall
require the concerned
officials of the Municipality
to establish and maintain a
GAD database containing
Sex-Disaggregated data and
gender statistics and include
in the preparation of the
GAD Planning and Budget in
compliance with
PCW/NEDA/ DBM Joint
Circular No. 2012-01.

The Municipal Mayor shall
Require the Disbursing
Officers to submit the Report
of Disbursements (ROD)
pertaining to their cash
advances together with all the
liquidating instruments every
5th day of the ensuing month
pursuant to Section 46 and
Annex 46 of the NGAS for
LGUs; and the liquidating
instruments, in violation of
Sections 100 and
122, PD 1445.
5. Supply Officer to prepare and maintain complete and updated Property Cards for PPE and reconcile their records regularly with the Accounting Office.

11. We recommended that Management require the concerned officials of the Municipality to establish and maintain a GAD database containing Sex-Disaggregated data and statistics and include it in the preparation of the GAD Planning and Budget in compliance with PCW/NEDA/ DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01.

1.6 Therefore, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a) Instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct immediately an
extensive inventory of the road network system under the
Municipality’s jurisdiction and prepare a report thereon;
b) Require the Municipal Accountant to religiously update the
subsidiary records for roads and road components for every
road network based on the result of the physical count;
c) Further require the Municipal Accountant to prepare a lapsing
schedule for computation of the depreciation at the end of each
year;
d) Instruct the General Services Officer (GSO)/Supply Officer to
religiously maintain Local Road Inventory and Road Map;
e) Likewise, have the GSO/Supply Officer keep an updated
complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and
its components and make a reconciliation with that of the
Accounting records; and
f) Finally, require the Municipal Engineer to provide the
Accountant and the General Services Office with the complete
description and cost segregation of road components for road
projects.

2.8 Hence, we recommended that Management:
a) Immediately submit a written explanation on how the
procurement of goods pushed through and consequently paid
when ‘no meeting of minds’ took place as depicted by the
unsigned and deficiently filled-out POs; and
b) Henceforth, instruct that POs must be completely filled-out with essential
information acknowledged by the supplier before acceptance and payment
thereof.

3.1.8 Hence, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a) Cause the immediate submission of the copies of BAC
Resolution (if any) to the audit team for evaluation of the
mode of procurement availed;
b) If none, submit a written explanation on why were the BAC Resolutions not
prepared prior to notice of award.
c) Henceforth, instruct the BAC to strictly prepare the required BAC resolution
and have it approved by the LCE prior to the notice of award to promote
transparency.

3.2.6 Hence, we firmly recommended that the Local Chief
Executive:
a) Cause the immediate submission of the missing/lacking
RFQs, if any, to the audit team;
b) If none, submit a written explanation on noncompliance,
thereof.
c) Henceforth, strictly, follow the rules on RFQs as prescribed in IRR of RA
9184.

4.5 Hence, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) cause
the immediate submission of the approved supplemental AIP to the
audit team to facilitate assessment on the validity and propriety of
PPAs implemented.

5.5 In view of the noted deficiencies, we highly recommended that the
Local Chief Executive direct the Local Finance Committee:
a) To submit certified true copies of certifications of posting
requirements by the BAC Secretariat relative to the BGCM PPA
procurements;
b) Though late, still post the accumulated reports on the LGU’s
website or Facebook page and in conspicuous public places in the
locality for general transparency; and
c) Finally, furnish the audit team a copy of the written notice when
posting has been complied, including photos to validate posting in
conspicuous places in the locality.

6.6 Hence, we strongly recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a) Cause the immediate submission of all the deficient documents
stated herein.
b) Henceforth, strictly adhere to the rules and procedures
prescribed under IRR RA 9184 on agency to agency
procurements.

7.4 Hence, we recommended and Management agreed to:
a) Instruct the GAD Focal Person to observe timely submission of
GAD Plan and Budget to DILG Regional Office for review and
approval in compliance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01; and
b) Strictly ensure that the same shall be issued a DILG certificate of
approval upon return and before incorporation to the Annual
Budget to be enacted by the Sanggunian.

1. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct and supervise the Municipal Treasurer to conduct an intensive tax campaign by sending treasury personnel to the barangays especially during barangay assemblies to invite more taxpayers to pay on time and give emphasis on penalties and sanctions when noncompliant.

2. Require the OIC-Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to provide justification on the delayed completion of the above-mentioned projects and/or present any document like Suspension Order/Time Extension which must be duly approved by the Municipal Mayor; and

3. Ensure that all factors are considered during the planning and conception of the projects and proper mechanisms are put in place in the proper monitoring of the progress implementation of the projects.
4. Inventory committee to conduct the physical count of PPE of the Municipality and submit the RPCPPE
6. Require the Inventory Committee of the Municipality to conduct the inventory of PPEs subject for disposal and prepare and furnish the Office of the Auditor of the IIRUP; and
7. Dispose the unserviceable vehicles and heavy equipment in accordance with the steps and procedures cited under Section 79 of PD 1445.

8. Issue demand letters to notify the concerned officials and employees of their responsibility to settle their unliquidated cash advances. If settlement was not received, impose withholding of their salaries considering that they have been notified and given more adequate time to liquidate;
9. Strictly monitor the submission of liquidation reports to ensure timely accounting thereof and to avoid undue accumulation of outstanding cash advances; and
10. Strictly comply with the provisions of the abovementioned provisions.
12. Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents together with all the liquidating instruments to the office of the Auditor.

13. Impose the suspension of payment of salaries of the employees responsible for the delayed submission or non-submission of the required reports until the same shall have been complied with in pursuance to Section 122 of PD 1445.

14. We recommended that LGU exhausts all available remedies provided under existing laws, rules and regulations in enforcing immediate compliance with the audit suspensions pursuant to Section 7.1.1 of COA Circular No.2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

15. Require the Disbursing Officers to submit the Report of Disbursements (ROD) pertaining to their cash advances together with all the liquidating instruments every 5th day of the ensuing month pursuant to Section 46 and Annex 46 of the NGAS for LGUs;
16. Direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately review and evaluate the submitted RODs and its attachments and then prepare the Journal Entry Voucher for submission to the Auditor;
17. Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit the Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) on a monthly basis, and
18. Finally, due to the unreasonable delay, require the immediate submission of all the reports to the Office of the Auditor, and the submission of reports on a monthly basis

19. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant in cooperation with the Budget Officer prepare the SCBAA as required in PPSAS 24 and submit to COA immediately for audit and subsequent SCBAA should be submitted on time. We recommended that Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the Notes to Financial Statements in a timely manner for us to verify and cross reference some accounts in the main financial statements

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct and supervise the Municipal
Treasurer to conduct an intensive tax campaign by sending treasury personnel to the
barangays especially during barangay assemblies to invite more taxpayers to pay on
time and give emphasis on penalties and sanctions when noncompliant.

We recommended that Management
a. Require the OIC-Municipal Engineer, and Municipal Planning and
Development Coordinator (MPDC) to provide justification on the
delayed completion of the above-mentioned projects and/or
present any document like Suspension Order/Time Extension
which must be duly approved by the Municipal Mayor; and
b. Ensure that all factors are considered during the planning and
conception of the projects and proper mechanisms are put in
place in the proper monitoring of the progress implementation of
the projects.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Honorable
Municipal Mayor:
a. Issue an Executive Order creating the Inventory Committee of
the Municipality and direct immediately such committee to
conduct the physical count of PPE and submit the RPCPPE to
the Office of the Auditor; and
b. Require the General Services Office to maintain Property Cards
and reconcile their records on a regular basis with the
Accounting Office.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Require the Inventory Committee of the Municipality to conduct
the inventory of PPEs subject for disposal and prepare and
furnish the Office of the Auditor of the IIRUP; and
b. Dispose the unserviceable vehicles and heavy equipment in
accordance with the steps and procedures cited under Section
79 of PD 1445.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant
to:
a. Issue demand letters to notify the concerned officials and
employees of their responsibility to settle their respective
unliquidated cash advances. If settlement is disregarded, impose
withholding of their salaries considering that they have been
notified and given more adequate time to liquidate; and
b. Strictly monitor the submission of liquidation reports to ensure
timely accounting thereof and to avoid undue accumulation of
outstanding cash advances.

We recommended that Management require the concerned officials of
the Municipality to establish and maintain a GAD database containing
Sex-Disaggregated data and gender statistics and include it in the
preparation of the GAD Planning and Budget in compliance with
PCW/NEDA/ DBM Joint Circular No. 2012-01.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the disbursement
vouchers and its supporting documents together with all the
liquidating instruments within the prescribed time to the office of
the Auditor.
b. Impose the suspension of payment of salaries to the employees
responsible for the delayed submission or non-submission of the
required reports until the same shall have been complied with in
pursuance to Section 122 of PD 1445.

Hence, we recommended that LGU exhaust all available remedies
provided under existing laws, rules and regulations in enforcing
immediate compliance with the audit suspensions pursuant to Section
7.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

1. We recommended that Management direct the Office of the Municipal Engineer to reevaluate the observations noted by the Inspectorate Team of the Technical Services Office (TSO) of the COA-ARMM per their Inspection Report for Infrastructure Projects dated October 29, 2018.

2. We recommended that the Head of Agency require the Municipal Engineer to supply the necessary information in the billboard being posted in the location site as provided in Annex A (attached for ready reference) pursuant to paragraph 2.2.3 of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

3. We recommended that Management require the concerned division for the submission of monthly Project Monitoring Report in order for us in coordination with Technical Specialist of COA-ARMM to validate the status of work accomplished in compliance with the suggested frequency of verification as provided for in the same circular.

4. We recommended that Management direct the BAC Secretariat and other concerned division officials to furnish the copies of the contracts and purchase orders to the Auditor within five (5) days after its necessary supporting documents pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.
5. We further recommended that Management require the concerned officials to immediately submit all the perfected contracts and purchase orders especially

6. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant in cooperation with the Budget Officer prepare the SCBAA as required in PPSAS 24 and submit to COA immediately for audit and subsequent SCBAA should be submitted on time.

7. We recommended that Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the Notes to Financial Statements in a timely manner for us to verify and cross-reference some accounts in the main financial statements.

8. Require the Disbursing Officers to submit the Report of Disbursements (ROD) pertaining to their cash advances together with all the liquidating instruments every 5th day of the ensuing month pursuant to Section 46 and Annex 46 of the NGAS for LGUs;
9. Direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately review and evaluate the submitted RODs and its attachments and then prepare the Journal Entry Voucher for submission to the Auditor;
10. Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit the Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) on a monthly basis, and
11. Finally, due to the unreasonable delay, require the immediate submission of all the reports to the Office of the Auditor, and the submission of reports on a monthly basis.

12. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant in cooperation with the Budget Officer prepare the SCBAA as required in PPSAS 24 and submit to COA immediately for audit and subsequent SCBAA should be submitted on time.

13. We recommended that Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the Notes to Financial Statements in a timely manner for us to verify and cross-reference some accounts in the main financial statements.

14. We recommended that Management exhaust all available remedies provided under existing laws, rules and regulations in enforcing immediate compliance with the audit suspensions pursuant to Section 7.1.1 of COA Circular No.2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommended that Management direct the Office of the Municipal
Engineer reevaluate the observations noted by the Inspectorate Team and
submit justification or explanation on the variance of accomplishments
particularly items V, VI and VII as found in details in the Inspection
Report.

We recommended that the Head of the Agency require the Municipal
Engineer to supply the necessary information in the billboard being
posted in the location site as provided in Annex A (attached for ready
reference) and pursuant to paragraph 2.2.3 of COA Circular No. 2013-
004 dated January 30, 2013.

We recommended that Management require the concerned division for
the submission of monthly Project Monitoring Report in order for us in
coordination with Technical Specialist of COA-ARMM to validate the
status of work accomplished in compliance with the suggested frequency
of verification as provided for in the same circular.

We recommended that Management direct the BAC Secretariat and
other concerned division officials to furnish the copies of the contracts
and purchase orders to the Auditor within five (5) days after its
perfection together with all its necessary supporting documents pursuant
COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We further recommended that Management require the concerned
officials to immediately submit all the perfected contracts and purchase
orders especially those on-going projects which are partially implemented
in the months of July to December 2018 for us to conduct the necessary
legal and auditorial review.

We recommended that Management direct the Local Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Officer (LDDRMO) to prepare and submit
Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan
(LDRRMFIP) based on the LDRRM Plan and Budget approved by the
LDDRM Council.

We also recommended that Management direct the Municipal
Accountant in coordination with the MDRRMO to prepare and submit
the monthly Report on Utilization of DRRMF (Form attached for ready
reference) in compliance with the above quoted rules.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor

a) Require the Disbursing Officers to submit the Report of
Disbursements (ROD) pertaining to their cash advances together
with all the supporting liquidating instruments every 5th day of the
ensuing month pursuant to Section 46 and Annex 46 of the NGAS
for LGUs.

b) Direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately review and
evaluate the submitted RODs and its attachments and then prepare
the Journal Entry Vouchers for submission to the Auditor.

c) Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit the Report of
Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) on a monthly basis,

and
d) Finally, due to the unreasonable delay, require the immediate
submission of all the reports to the Office of the Auditor, and the
submission of the said reports on a monthly basis.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant in cooperation with the
Budget Officer prepare the SCBAA as required in PPSAS 24 and submit
to COA immediately for audit and subsequent SCBAA should be
submitted on time.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to prepare and submit the Notes to Financial Statements on
time for us to verify and cross reference some accounts in the main
financial statements.

We recommended that Management exhaust all available remedies
provided under existing laws, rules and regulations in enforcing
immediate compliance with the audit suspensions pursuant to Section
7.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.
